suggestions.title: Keyboard shortcut sets ##office double-hash in URL
suggestions.description: In App.jsx lines 604–605, the keyboard handler assigns `window.location.hash = '#office'` and `window.location.hash = '#dashboard'` — strings that already include '#'. The browser prepends another '#', producing '##office' in the URL. The hashchange listener then reads `window.location.hash.slice(1)` → '#office', which fails the equality check against 'office', breaking browser back/forward tab restoration. The tab-button click handlers on lines 887 and 900 correctly use plain strings without '#'. Fix: remove the '#' prefix from both keyboard shortcut assignments to match the click handlers.

suggestions.title: task-tag spans are mouse-only — keyboard users cannot filter by tag
suggestions.description: Task-tag spans in WorkerCard (App.jsx lines 278–290) have onClick and cursor:pointer styling but no tabIndex, no role='button', and no onKeyDown handler. Keyboard-only users and screen reader users cannot activate tag filters at all. Fix: add tabIndex={0}, role='button', and an onKeyDown handler that calls onTagClick on Enter and Space. Also add a focus-visible outline style in App.css for .task-tag to match the hover appearance.

suggestions.title: Clickable Character divs in Office.jsx lack role and keyboard activation
suggestions.description: The Character component (Office.jsx line 374) renders a <div> with tabIndex={0} and an onClick when onWorkerClick is provided, but has no role attribute and no onKeyDown handler. Screen readers announce it as a generic group with no hint that it is interactive, and pressing Enter or Space does nothing. Fix: when handleClick is defined, add role='button' to the div and an onKeyDown that calls handleClick on Enter/Space. The aria-label could reuse the worker name + role string.

suggestions.title: ProgressBar milestone Set never resets — flash won't fire on task restart
suggestions.description: In ProgressBar (App.jsx ~line 173), milestonesRef holds a Set that accumulates milestones (25, 50, 75, 100) but is never cleared. When a worker's progress resets to 0 for a new task, all milestones are already in the Set so the flash animations never trigger again for the lifetime of the component. Fix: add a second effect (or extend the existing one) that detects when progress drops below a previously-seen milestone — compare against a prevProgressRef — and clears milestonesRef when a reset is detected.

suggestions.title: w.started is undefined — stuck-worker detection never triggers in StatsBar
suggestions.description: In StatsBar (App.jsx line 345), stuckWorkers filters by `w.started` but worker objects from the API use `updated_at`, not `started`. `new Date(undefined).getTime()` returns NaN, making the `> 7_200_000` comparison always false — the Stuck stat card never appears. Line 400 then calls formatDuration(stuckWorkers[0].started) which returns null, producing an empty label. Fix: replace `w.started` with `w.updated_at` throughout the stuck-worker logic, or add a `started` field to the worker data schema.

suggestions.title: activeWorkers array is not memoized — triggers 5+ effects on every render
suggestions.description: In App.jsx (line 637), activeWorkers is computed with a plain .filter() call outside of useMemo, creating a new array reference on every render. Five downstream useEffects (cinematic entry/exit diff, vibe streak, favicon, tab title, and the confetti logic) and multiple child component props all depend on this reference, causing unnecessary re-renders and effect re-executions whenever any unrelated state (e.g. theme, tab, focusedWorker) changes. Fix: wrap in useMemo with [allWorkers] as the dependency.

suggestions.title: Keyboard shortcuts (1, 2, R) are completely undiscoverable
suggestions.description: The keyboard shortcuts 1 (Office tab), 2 (Dashboard tab), and R (refresh) are implemented but only surfaced as tooltip `title` attributes on the tab buttons and sync button. There is no help tooltip, legend, or any visible hint that these shortcuts exist. Power users who discover them by accident love them; everyone else never knows. Fix: add a small '?' help icon in the header-right area that, on click or focus, shows a keyboard shortcut popover. Alternatively, append the shortcut hint directly to the tab button label in parentheses, e.g. 'Office (1)'.

suggestions.title: Mobile layout has large dead space below the office floor
suggestions.description: On 375px wide screens (see screenshot-mobile.png), the office floor renders at roughly 335x251px due to the aspect-ratio:4/3 constraint, leaving over 200px of empty dark background below the floor before the footer. The idle-worker wandering area and plants are too small to read. The lower third of the office floor itself is also mostly empty (no desks, just entrance icon and scattered plants). Fix: on mobile breakpoints (<480px), either switch the office-floor to a taller aspect-ratio (e.g. 3/4 or 1/1) and redistribute desk positions, or show a condensed 'roster list' view instead of the spatial layout.

suggestions.title: App.jsx is 1027 lines — monolithic file should be split into components
suggestions.description: App.jsx contains at least 8 distinct components (ActivityLogEntry, ActivityLog, StatusBadge, ProgressBar, WorkerCard, DeltaBadge, StatsBar, EmptyState, LoadingSkeleton) plus the main App, all utility functions, and all constants — 1027 lines total. This makes the file hard to navigate, increases cognitive load for contributors, and slows IDE tooling. Fix: extract each component into its own file under src/components/ (e.g., WorkerCard.jsx, StatsBar.jsx, ActivityLog.jsx) and move getRelativeTime/formatFullDateTime/getTaskTags to src/utils/format.js.

suggestions.title: WORKER_ROLE_COLORS in App.jsx duplicates and diverges from ROLE_COLORS in constants.js
suggestions.description: App.jsx (line 14) defines WORKER_ROLE_COLORS keyed by worker name (e.g. Maya: '#a855f7'), while constants.js ROLE_COLORS is keyed by role string (e.g. 'Frontend Engineer': '#a78bfa'). Both are used for colors, but they assign different hex values — Maya's confetti uses #a855f7 while her worker card accent uses #a78bfa — creating subtle but real visual inconsistency. WORKER_ROLE_COLORS also needs manual updating whenever the roster changes. Fix: remove WORKER_ROLE_COLORS from App.jsx; derive confetti color from ROLE_COLORS using the worker's role field, falling back to a default accent color.

suggestions.title: completionToast should use role=alert instead of aria-live=polite
suggestions.description: The completion toast (App.jsx line 809) uses aria-live='polite', which defers screen reader announcements until the current interaction is idle. For a transient 3-second celebration notification, this means screen reader users may miss it entirely if they are actively interacting with the page. The equivalent sighted UX is seeing confetti + toast simultaneously — the announcement should be equally immediate. Fix: change aria-live='polite' to role='alert' (which implies aria-live='assertive') so the announcement is made immediately on insertion.

suggestions.title: getRelativeTime duplicated — defined in App.jsx and re-inlined in Office.jsx
suggestions.description: App.jsx defines getRelativeTime at line 34. Office.jsx (lines 394–403) contains an identical inline implementation inside the ghost-bubble IIFE instead of importing the shared function. Two sources of truth means any bug fix or format change (e.g. adding 'weeks ago') must be applied in two places. Fix: move getRelativeTime (and formatFullDateTime) to src/utils/time.js, export them, and import in both App.jsx and Office.jsx.

suggestions.title: Vibe streak persists in sessionStorage only — resets on every new browser tab
suggestions.description: The vibe streak counter (App.jsx line 710) reads and writes to sessionStorage, which is scoped to a single browser tab. Opening a new tab or restarting the browser resets the streak to 1 regardless of how many consecutive polls have kept the same vibe. The streak-based office plant growth (Office.jsx line 511) is therefore never seen beyond the 'seedling' stage in practice. Fix: switch to localStorage (with the same key 'bloberto-vibe-streak') so the streak persists across sessions, and add a streak expiry mechanism (e.g. reset if last-updated timestamp is more than 2 hours ago).

suggestions.title: Office.css character animations lack prefers-reduced-motion guard
suggestions.description: App.css has a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block that correctly disables card/skeleton animations. However, Office.css defines at least 8 animations (enter-office, char-bob, wander-1 through wander-4, char-glitch, fade-in-char, deep-work-pulse) with no equivalent reduced-motion guard. Users who have requested reduced motion in their OS settings will still see all character movements, bobbing, glitch effects, and wander paths. Fix: add a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block to Office.css that sets animation: none !important and transition: none !important on .char, .char--working, .char--idle, .char--glitch, and .char--deep-work.
